name: Embalse de la Fuensanta y río Tús desde Molinicos — circular por el Parque Natural de Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Molinicos traverse the dramatic karstic landscapes of the Sierra del Segura, within the Parque Natural de los Calares del Río Mundo y de la Sima. The region features impressive limestone formations, including the Source of the Mundo River with its waterfalls, and rolling hills. Cyclists encounter quiet roads winding through pine and holm oak forests, olive groves, and varied terrain with both gentle and sustained climbs. The area offers a blend of serene natural beauty and challenging routes. Best touring cycling routes around Molinicos The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de la Fuensanta y río Tús desde Molinicos — circular por el Parque Natural de Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima, a 32.7 miles (52.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers scenic views of the Fuensanta reservoir and the Tus River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Riópar Viejo Spring – Plaza de Luis Escudero loop from Riópar, a difficult 22.5 miles (36.2 km) path. This route features varied terrain and passes through the natural beauty surrounding Riópar. Local touring cyclists also love the Mundo River crossing – Puerto de las Crucetillas loop from Riópar, a 20.6 miles (33.2 km) trail leading through the Mundo River valley and over Puerto de las Crucetillas, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Touring cycling around Molinicos is defined by karstic formations, river valleys, and dense woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from rolling climbs to more challenging ascents. The routes in Molinicos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 9 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Molinicos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Molinicos?
A: Molinicos offers a wide array of touring cycling routes, with over 30 options available on komoot. These routes traverse the stunning landscapes of the Sierra del Segura, providing diverse experiences for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Molinicos?
A: The terrain around Molinicos is characterized by dramatic karstic landscapes, rolling hills, and river valleys. You'll encounter quiet roads winding through pine and holm oak forests, olive groves, and varied surfaces. While there are no 'monster alpine passes,' routes feature both gentle and sustained climbs, offering a blend of serene natural beauty and challenging sections.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly touring cycling routes in Molinicos?
A: While many routes in Molinicos are rated as difficult due to significant elevation gain, the region also offers options with more rolling, gentle climbs suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride. For example, some routes focus on the valleys or connect smaller villages, providing a more relaxed experience compared to the longer, more challenging ascents.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Molinicos area?
A: Yes, Molinicos is well-suited for circular touring cycling routes. Many tours are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363404784/embalse-de-la-fuensanta-y-rio-tus-desde-molinicos-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-los-calares-del-mundo-y-de-la-sima" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Fuensanta y río Tús desde Molinicos — circular por el Parque Natural de Los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views of the Fuensanta reservoir and the Tus River as a loop.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while cycling in Molinicos?
A: Molinicos is rich in natural beauty. Along your cycling routes, you can encounter impressive karstic formations within the Parque Natural de los Calares del Río Mundo y de la Sima. Notable sights include the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5318691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Peak&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3484172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Landscapes Around Riópar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in Molinicos?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, families can find more relaxed options by focusing on flatter sections along river valleys or shorter loops between villages. It's advisable to check the elevation profiles and distances of specific routes to ensure they match your family's ability level. The region's quiet roads can be ideal for a peaceful family ride.

Q: Is Molinicos suitable for cycling with dogs?
A: The natural environment of Molinicos, with its extensive forests and quiet roads, can be suitable for cycling with dogs, provided they are well-behaved and accustomed to the activity. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash where required, especially within the Parque Natural de los Calares del Río Mundo y de la Sima, and be mindful of local wildlife and livestock. Carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Molinicos?
A: Molinicos is generally enjoyable for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winters are cooler but can still offer pleasant cycling days, though some higher elevation routes might be affected by colder weather.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Molinicos?
A: In Molinicos and surrounding villages like Riópar, you can typically find public parking areas. It's recommended to park in designated village car parks or along streets where parking is permitted. Always check local signage for any restrictions. Starting points for many routes are often accessible from these central locations.

Q: What do other cyclists say about their experience touring cycling in Molinicos?
A: The touring cycling routes around Molinicos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning scenery, peaceful roads, and the sense of adventure the region offers. The 'rolling, gentle climbs through quiet forests, with sweeping views that appear out of nowhere' are frequently highlighted as a memorable aspect of the experience.

Q: Are there any specific challenges or considerations for touring cyclists in Molinicos?
A: Cyclists should be prepared for significant elevation changes, as many routes feature sustained climbs, even if not extremely steep. The roads are generally quiet, but some sections might be remote, so carrying sufficient water, snacks, and basic repair tools is advisable. The karstic terrain can also mean varied road surfaces, so a touring bike capable of handling different conditions is recommended.

Q: Can I rent a touring bicycle in Molinicos?
A: While Molinicos is gaining recognition as a cycling destination, dedicated touring bicycle rental shops might be limited directly within the small town. It's advisable to check with local tourism offices or larger towns in the Albacete province for rental options if you plan to rent a bike for your trip.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay along the touring cycling routes?
A: Molinicos and the surrounding villages offer authentic hospitality with local cafes, pubs, and rural accommodations. While some routes might pass through more remote areas, you'll find opportunities to refuel and rest in the towns and hamlets along the way. It's a good idea to plan your route with potential stops in mind, especially for longer rides.

Q: What makes Molinicos an 'underrated cycling gem'?
A: Molinicos is considered an 'underrated cycling gem' due to its combination of stunning natural landscapes, quiet roads, and a genuine sense of adventure, all within the beautiful Sierra del Segura. It offers a less crowded and more authentic cycling experience compared to more well-known regions, with affordable travel and a rich cultural backdrop, making it a rewarding destination for touring cyclists.
tips: It is a charming town located east of the mountains. It is also known in the cycling world for the number of races that are held.
Nice bridge over the Tús river that feeds the Fuensanta reservoir. Stop for a while to take some photos of the turquoise waters of the swamp.
Beautiful suspension bridge over the Tús River, it is in perfect condition. You have to dismount to cross it, especially if you meet hikers.
